title: fusermount in container fails with "operation not permitted"
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I am trying to use fusermount inside a podman container running on a fedora 36 host. Despite having added /dev/fuse to the container, the command fails saying "Operation not permitted". Am I missing something here or is this a bug?

I've observed the behavior in rootless and rootful containers. The error is probably not related to SELinux because setting it to permissive did not help. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
buildah from fedora
buildah run fedora-working-container dnf install sshfs -y
buildah commit fedora-working-container sshfs
podman run -it --rm --device /dev/fuse sshfs sshfs remote:/ /mnt
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Mounting fails with the following error: `fusermount3: mount failed: Operation not permitted`


**Describe the results you expected:**
Mounting works, no error is shown. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Fri May  6 18:15:54 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
> fusermount3: mount failed: Operation not permitted

This smells like the seccomp profile is blocking the syscall. It should work when using `--security-opt  seccomp=unconfined`.  Note that this will turn off seccomp entirely.

@vrothberg I tried disabling seccomp as you suggested, but keep getting the same error message:
```
$ podman run -it --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --device /dev/fuse sshfs sshfs remote:/ /mnt
fusermount3: mount failed: Operation not permitted
```
I was to hasty. Can you retry with `--privileged`?
Thanks, with `--privileged` I was able to get rid of the error. Are there any side-effects one should be aware of when using that flag?

> Thanks, with `--privileged` I was able to get rid of the error. Are there any side-effects one should be aware of when using that flag?

It will effectively disable most security mechanisms.  I recommend having a look at a great blog post from @rhatdan on how to diagnose such permission errors: https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/container-permission-denied-errors
